```r
# Ensure Close is numeric (remove any non‑numeric characters just in case)

  mutate(btc_ret_30d = log(Close / lag(Close, 30)))             # 30‑day log return
```

run a regression of bitcoin 30 day return on spx 30 day return. make sure to add a lag so we're not using information we dont have yet, only relying on yesterdays spx to predict todays bitcoin return

### Convert the `Date` columns in both datasets to `Date` class  

```r
bitcoin_daily_5y <- bitcoin_daily_5y %>% 
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))

spx_daily_5y <- spx_daily_5y %>% 
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))
```

[R EXECUTION RESULT]
Success: True

[ENV] bitcoin_daily_5y: 1825x8, spx_daily_5y: 1254x6

### Compute the 30‑day log return for Bitcoin and store it as `btc_ret_30d`

```r
# Ensure Close is numeric (remove any non‑numeric characters just in case)
bitcoin_daily_5y <- bitcoin_daily_5y %>%
  mutate(Close = as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.eE-]", "", Close))) %>%   # clean if needed
  arrange(Date) %>%                                             # make sure dates are ordered
  mutate(btc_ret_30d = log(Close / lag(Close, 30)))             # 30‑day log return
```

[R EXECUTION RESULT]
Success: True

[ENV] bitcoin_daily_5y: 1825x9, spx_daily_5y: 1254x6

### Compute the 30‑day log return for the S&P 500 and store it as `spx_ret_30d`

```r
spx_daily_5y <- spx_daily_5y %>%
  # Clean the Close column just in case it was imported as character
  mutate(Close = as.numeric(gsub("[^0-9.eE-]", "", Close))) %>%
  arrange(Date) %>%                                   # ensure chronological order
  mutate(spx_ret_30d = log(Close / lag(Close, 30)))   # 30‑day log return
```

[R EXECUTION RESULT]
Success: True

[ENV] bitcoin_daily_5y: 1825x9, spx_daily_5y: 1254x7

### Add a one‑day lag of the 30‑day S&P 500 return (yesterday’s SPX return)  

```r
spx_daily_5y <- spx_daily_5y %>%
  arrange(Date) %>%                     # make sure dates are in chronological order
  mutate(spx_ret_30d_lag1 = lag(spx_ret_30d, 1))
```

[R EXECUTION RESULT]
Success: True

[ENV] bitcoin_daily_5y: 1825x9, spx_daily_5y: 1254x8

### Keep only the columns required for the regression (Date and the two return variables)

```r
btc_subset <- bitcoin_daily_5y %>%
  select(Date, btc_ret_30d)

spx_subset <- spx_daily_5y %>%
  select(Date, spx_ret_30d_lag1)
```

[R EXECUTION RESULT]
Success: True

[ENV] bitcoin_daily_5y: 1825x9, btc_subset: 1825x2, spx_daily_5y: 1254x8, spx_subset: 1254x2

### Join the Bitcoin and S&P 500 return subsets on the common `Date` column and drop any rows that still contain missing values

```r
btc_spx_data <- btc_subset %>%
  inner_join(spx_subset, by = "Date") %>%
  drop_na(btc_ret_30d, spx_ret_30d_lag1)
```

This creates a new data frame `btc_spx_data` that contains only dates for which we have both the 30‑day Bitcoin return and the lagged 30‑day S&P 500 return, ready for the regression in the next step.

[R EXECUTION RESULT]

[ENV] bitcoin_daily_5y: 1825x9, btc_spx_data: 1223x3, btc_subset: 1825x2, spx_daily_5y: 1254x8, spx_subset: 1254x2

### Run the regression of Bitcoin 30‑day return on yesterday’s S&P 500 30‑day return and clean up temporary objects

```r
# Linear model: Bitcoin 30‑day log return ~ lagged SPX 30‑day log return
btc_spx_reg <- lm(btc_ret_30d ~ spx_ret_30d_lag1, data = btc_spx_data)

# Remove intermediate data frames that are no longer needed
rm(btc_subset, spx_subset, btc_spx_data)
